description_long: This object consists of a cluster of four mushrooms with slender, pale stems and smooth, conical caps. The caps are a rich ochre-brown color and have a matte, slightly textured surface. The stems are thin, cylindrical, and off-white, branching from a common base and curving gently upward. The caps are unadorned, lacking visible spots or scales, and their margins are smooth and slightly curved. No gills are visible from any angle, as the caps are viewed primarily from the side and above. The overall appearance suggests a natural, organic form that could be found in a park or green urban space.
description: A cluster of four ochre-brown, smooth-capped mushrooms with slender, pale stems branching from a common base. The caps are conical and matte, with no visible gills or ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four mushrooms, with three caps clearly visible and one partially obscured, highlighting the cluster formation.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the tallest mushroom and the curvature of the stems, with two caps mostly overlapping.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the caps and the convergence of the stems at the base, with all four caps visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arrangement of the caps from a different angle, with the stems' curvature more pronounced.
